_comment: Nuclear-medicine radionuclide data for shielding (I-131, Tc-99m, F-18, Lu-177) and the I-131 patient-as-source biokinetics. Shielding uses broad-beam tenth-value layers (TVL): B = 10^(-x/TVL), x in mm. Sources: OUMANO2025 (TVL/HVL, dose-rate constants), RG839 (I-131 biokinetics), TG108 (PET method). Every value editable.
exposure_rate_constant_units: R*cm^2/(mCi*h)  [equivalently R/(mCi*h) at 1 cm]. Convert to air-kerma rate: 1 R ~ 8.76e-3 Gy(air). For ambient dose-equivalent screening, 1 R ~ 10 mSv is a common conservative approximation.

I-131.label: Iodine-131 (radioiodine therapy)
I-131.gamma_note: Principal photon 364 keV (81.5%); also 637 keV (7%), 284 keV (6%).

F-18.label: Fluorine-18 (PET / PET-CT, 511 keV annihilation photons)
F-18.gamma_note: Two 511 keV annihilation photons per decay (~193.5% incl. positron branching).
F-18.dose_rate_constant_ref: OUMANO2025; consistent with AAPM TG-108 (Gamma ~ 5.9 R*cm^2/mCi*h at 511 keV).

Lu-177.label: Lutetium-177 (PRRT / radioligand therapy)
Lu-177.gamma_note: Low-abundance gammas 113 keV (6.2%), 208 keV (11%); predominantly a beta emitter.

i131_biokinetics._comment: RG 8.39 Appendix B patient-as-source model for I-131. Integrated dose to a nearby person to total decay. D(inf) = 34.6 * Gamma * Q0 * E * {0.8*(1 - exp(-0.693*0.33/Tp)) + exp(-0.693*0.33/Tp)*[F1*T1eff + F2*T2eff]} / r^2, with Q0 in mCi, r in cm, T in days, Gamma in R*cm^2/mCi*h; result in R (~ rem ~ 10 mSv/R). First 8 h (0.33 d): 80% at physical half-life.
i131_biokinetics.integration_constant_34_6: 34.6 = 24 h/day * 1.4427 (1/ln2), integrating an exponentially decaying point source over time.
i131_biokinetics.occupancy_note: RG 8.39: E=0.25 at 1 m default (conservative); 0.75 for <1 day component; 0.125 if patient-specific ALARA instructions justify it.

pet_patient_source_tg108._comment: AAPM TG-108 PET/CT patient-as-source method. Patient becomes the source after injection. Account for uptake phase (pre-scan) and imaging phase with decay; apply patient self-attenuation. Dose at distance = decayed, self-attenuated point source integrated over each phase.
pet_patient_source_tg108.patient_self_attenuation_note: Fraction of 511 keV fluence escaping the patient (TG-108); ~0.85 sometimes used pre-void. 0.36 accounts for tissue attenuation of annihilation photons.
pet_patient_source_tg108.dose_rate_constant_note: Air-kerma rate constant for F-18 ~0.092 uGy*m^2/(MBq*h) before patient attenuation (TG-108).
